concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.e…23 tokens truncated…
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true

jobs:
  checks:
    name: Check scope-cli
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master
        with:
          toolchain: 1.94.0
          components: rustfmt, clippy

      - name: Cache Rust build outputs
        uses: actions/cache@v6
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/git
            ~/.cargo/registry
            cli/target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cli-cargo-${{ hashFiles('cli/Cargo.lock') }}-${{ github.sha }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-cli-cargo-${{ hashFiles('cli/Cargo.lock') }}
            ${{ runner.os }}-cli-cargo-

      - name: Check formatting
        run: cargo fmt --manifest-path cli/Cargo.toml -- --check

      - name: Test
        run: cargo test --manifest-path cli/Cargo.toml --locked

      - name: Lint
        run: cargo clippy --manifest-path cli/Cargo.toml --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings

      - name: Build release binaries
        if: github.event_name != 'pull_request' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        run: >
          cargo build
          --manifest-path cli/Cargo.toml
          --release
          --locked
          --bin scope
          --bin scope-cli-service

  build:
    name: Build ${{ matrix.label }}
    needs: checks
    if: github.event_name != 'pull_request' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - label: Linux x64
            runner: ubuntu-latest
            target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
            artifact: scope-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
            binary: target/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/release/scope
            builder: cargo
            smoke: true
          - label: Linux ARM64
            runner: ubuntu-latest
            target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
            artifact: scope-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
            binary: target/aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu/release/scope
            builder: cross
            smoke: false
          - label: macOS Intel
            runner: macos-15-intel
            target: x86_64-apple-darwin
            artifact: scope-x86_64-apple-darwin
            binary: target/x86_64-apple-darwin/release/scope
            builder: cargo
            smoke: true
          - label: macOS Apple Silicon
            runner: macos-15
            target: aarch64-apple-darwin
            artifact: scope-aarch64-apple-darwin
            binary: target/aarch64-apple-darwin/release/scope
            builder: cargo
            smoke: true
          - label: Windows x64
            runner: windows-latest
            target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
            artifact: scope-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.exe
            binary: target/x86_64-pc-windows-msvc/release/scope.exe
            builder: cargo
            smoke: true
          - label: Windows ARM64
            runner: windows-latest
            target: aarch64-pc-windows-msvc
            artifact: scope-aarch64-pc-windows-msvc.exe
            binary: target/aarch64-pc-windows-msvc/release/scope.exe
            builder: cargo
            smoke: false

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master
        with:
          toolchain: 1.94.0
          targets: ${{ matrix.target }}

      - name: Cache Rust build outputs
        uses: actions/cache@v6
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/git
            ~/.cargo/registry
            cli/target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-cargo-${{ hashFiles('cli/Cargo.lock') }}-${{ github.sha }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-cargo-${{ hashFiles('cli/Cargo.lock') }}
            ${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-cargo-

      - name: Install cross
        if: matrix.builder == 'cross'
        working-directory: cli
        env:
          CARGO_TARGET_DIR: target/cargo-install
        run: cargo install cross --locked

      - name: Build scope
        working-directory: cli
        shell: bash
        run: |
          if [ "${{ matrix.builder }}" = "cross" ]; then
            cross build --release --locked --target "${{ matrix.target }}" --bin scope
          else
            cargo build --release --locked --target "${{ matrix.target }}" --bin scope
          fi

      - name: Smoke test
        if: matrix.smoke
        working-directory: cli
        shell: bash
        run: ./${{ matrix.binary }} --help

      - name: Stage artifact
        working-directory: cli
        shell: bash
        run: |
          mkdir -p dist
          cp "${{ matrix.binary }}" "dist/${{ matrix.artifact }}"

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
        with:
          name: ${{ matrix.artifact }}
          path: cli/dist/${{ matrix.artifact }}
          if-no-files-found: error

  deploy:
    name: Deploy scope-cli
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name != 'pull_request' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - name: Download artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v8
        with:
          path: cli/dist
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: Verify artifacts and write checksums
        shell: bash
        run: |
          expected=(
            scope-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
            scope-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
            scope-x86_64-apple-darwin
            scope-aarch64-apple-darwin
            scope-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.exe
            scope-aarch64-pc-windows-msvc.exe
          )

          for artifact in "${expected[@]}"; do
            test -s "cli/dist/$artifact"
          done

          (
            cd cli/dist
            for artifact in "${expected[@]}"; do
              sha256sum "$artifact" > "$artifact.sha256"
            done
          )

      - name: Install Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: 24

      - name: Stage Railway upload root
        shell: bash
        run: |
          rm -rf .railway-upload
          mkdir -p .railway-upload
          cp -a cli/. .railway-upload/
          cp rust-toolchain.toml .railway-upload/rust-toolchain.toml
          mkdir -p .railway-upload/crates
          cp -a crates/scope-core .railway-upload/crates/scope-core
          perl -0pi -e 's#path = "\.\./crates/scope-core"#path = "crates/scope-core"#' .railway-upload/Cargo.toml
          mkdir -p .railway-upload/cli
          cp -a cli/. .railway-upload/cli/
          mkdir -p .railway-upload/cli/crates
          cp rust-toolchain.toml .railway-upload/cli/rust-toolchain.toml
          cp -a crates/scope-core .railway-upload/cli/crates/scope-core
          perl -0pi -e 's#path = "\.\./crates/scope-core"#path = "crates/scope-core"#' .railway-upload/cli/Cargo.toml
          for root in .railway-upload .railway-upload/cli; do
            grep -F 'path = "crates/scope-core"' "$root/Cargo.toml"
            test -s "$root/railpack.json"
            test -s "$root/crates/scope-core/Cargo.toml"
          done

      - name: Install Railway CLI
        run: npm install -g @railway/cli@5.23.1

      - name: Deploy artifacts to Railway
        shell: bash
        env:
          RAILWAY_DEPLOY_MESSAGE: ${{ github.event.head_commit.message || github.workflow }}
          RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID: ${{ secrets.RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID }}
          RAILWAY_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.RAILWAY_TOKEN }}
        run: bash .github/scripts/deploy-railway.sh scope-cli .railway-upload
FILE .github/workflows/scope-web-ci.yml
name: Scope Web CI

on:
  pull_request:
    paths:
      - ".github/scripts/deploy-railway.sh"
      - ".github/workflows/scope-web-ci.yml"
      - "web/**"
  push:
    branches:
      - main
    paths:
      - ".github/scripts/deploy-railway.sh"
      - ".github/workflows/scope-web-ci.yml"
      - "web/**"
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

permissions:
  contents: read

env:
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true

jobs:
  checks:
    name: Check scope-web
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: web
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - name: Install Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: 24

      - name: Enable pnpm
        run: |
          corepack enable
          corepack prepare pnpm@11.1.0 --activate

      - name: Locate pnpm store
        id: pnpm-store
        run: echo "path=$(pnpm store path --silent)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Cache pnpm store
        uses: actions/cache@v6
        with:
          path: ${{ steps.pnpm-store.outputs.path }}
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-web-pnpm-${{ hashFiles('web/pnpm-lock.yaml') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-web-pnpm-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Check line limit
        run: pnpm check:line-limit

      - name: Test
        run: pnpm test

      - name: Typecheck
        run: pnpm typecheck

      - name: React Doctor
        run: pnpm check:react-doctor

      - name: Build
        if: github.event_name != 'pull_request' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        run: pnpm build

  deploy:
    name: Deploy scope-web
    needs: checks
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name != 'pull_request' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - name: Install Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: 24

      - name: Stage Railway upload root
        shell: bash
        run: |
          rm -rf .railway-upload
          mkdir -p .railway-upload
          cp -a web/. .railway-upload/
          mkdir -p .railway-upload/web
          cp -a web/. .railway-upload/web/

      - name: Install Railway CLI
        run: npm install -g @railway/cli@5.23.1

      - name: Deploy to Railway
        shell: bash
        env:
          RAILWAY_DEPLOY_MESSAGE: ${{ github.event.head_commit.message || github.workflow }}
          RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID: ${{ secrets.RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID }}
          RAILWAY_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.RAILWAY_TOKEN }}
        run: bash .github/scripts/deploy-railway.sh scope-web .railway-upload
FILE .github/workflows/scope-worker-ci.yml
name: Scope Worker CI

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
    paths:
      - ".github/scripts/deploy-railway.sh"
      - ".github/workflows/scope-worker-ci.yml"
      - "Cargo.lock"
      - "Cargo.toml"
      - "crates/scope-core/**"
      - "worker/**"
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: false

permissions:
  contents: read

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true

jobs:
  checks:
    name: Check scope-worker
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master
        with:
          toolchain: 1.94.0
          components: rustfmt, clippy

      - name: Cache Rust build outputs
        uses: actions/cache@v6
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/git
            ~/.cargo/registry
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-worker-cargo-${{ hashFiles('Cargo.lock') }}-${{ github.sha }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-worker-cargo-${{ hashFiles('Cargo.lock') }}
            ${{ runner.os }}-worker-cargo-

      - name: Check formatting
        run: cargo fmt --all --check

      - name: Test
        run: cargo test -p scope-core -p worker --locked

      - name: Lint
        run: cargo clippy -p scope-core -p worker --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings

  deploy:
    name: Deploy scope-worker
    needs: checks
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name != 'pull_request' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v7

      - name: Install Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: 24

      - name: Stage Railway upload root
        shell: bash
        run: |
          rm -rf .railway-upload
          mkdir -p .railway-upload
          cp Cargo.toml Cargo.lock rust-toolchain.toml .railway-upload/
          cp worker/railpack.json .railway-upload/railpack.json
          cp -a api .railway-upload/api
          cp -a worker .railway-upload/worker
          printf '%s\n' "${GITHUB_SHA:-manual}-${GITHUB_RUN_ID:-local}-${GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT:-0}" > .railway-upload/worker/railway-deploy-ref.txt
          cp -a crates .railway-upload/crates

      - name: Install Railway CLI
        run: npm install -g @railway/cli@5.23.1

      - name: Deploy to Railway
        shell: bash
        env:
          RAILWAY_DEPLOY_MESSAGE: ${{ github.event.head_commit.message || github.workflow }}
          RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID: ${{ secrets.RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID }}
          RAILWAY_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.RAILWAY_TOKEN }}
        run: bash .github/scripts/deploy-railway.sh scope-worker .railway-upload
FILE .github/scripts/deploy-railway.sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

service_name="${1:?usage: deploy-railway.sh <service-name> <upload-root>}"
upload_root="${2:?usage: deploy-railway.sh <service-name> <upload-root>}"

if [ -z "${RAILWAY_TOKEN:-}" ]; then
  echo "Set the RAILWAY_TOKEN repository secret before deploying ${service_name}."
  exit 1
fi

if [ -z "${RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID:-}" ]; then
  echo "Set the RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID repository secret before deploying ${service_name}."
  exit 1
fi

deploy_message_from_event() {
  local raw_message="${RAILWAY_DEPLOY_MESSAGE:-}"
  local first_line
  local pr_title

  first_line="$(printf '%s\n' "$raw_message" | sed -n '1p')"
  pr_title="$(printf '%s\n' "$raw_message" | awk 'NR > 1 && NF { print; exit }')"

  if [[ "$first_line" =~ ^Merge\ pull\ request\ #[0-9]+ ]] && [ -n "$pr_title" ]; then
    printf '%s\n' "$pr_title"
  elif [ -n "$first_line" ]; then
    printf '%s\n' "$first_line"
  else
    printf '%s\n' "${GITHUB_WORKFLOW:-Railway deploy}"
  fi
}

ensure_service_exists() {
  local service_name="$1"
  local services_json

  services_json="$(
    railway service list \
      --project "$RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID" \
      --environment production \
      --json
  )"

  if ! SERVICES_JSON="$services_json" SERVICE_NAME="$service_name" node -e 'const services = JSON.parse(process.env.SERVICES_JSON || "[]"); const name = process.env.SERVICE_NAME || ""; process.exit(services.some((service) => service.name === name || service.id === name) ? 0 : 1);'; then
    echo "Railway service '${service_name}' was not found in the production environment."
    echo "Create the service in Railway, configure its variables, then rerun this workflow."
    return 1
  fi
}

print_deployment_logs() {
  local service_name="$1"
  local deployment_id="$2"

  echo "::group::Railway build logs for ${service_name}/${deployment_id}"
  railway logs "$deployment_id" \
    --project "$RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID" \
    --service "$service_name" \
    --environment production \
    --build \
    --lines 200 || true
  echo "::endgroup::"

  echo "::group::Railway deploy logs for ${service_name}/${deployment_id}"
  railway logs "$deployment_id" \
    --project "$RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID" \
    --service "$service_name" \
    --environment production \
    --deployment \
    --lines 200 || true
  echo "::endgroup::"
}

service_health_line() {
  local service_name="$1"
  local services_json

  services_json="$(
    railway service list \
      --project "$RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID" \
      --environment production \
      --json
  )"

  SERVICES_JSON="$services_json" SERVICE_NAME="$service_name" node -e '
const services = JSON.parse(process.env.SERVICES_JSON || "[]");
const name = process.env.SERVICE_NAME || "";
const service = services.find((candidate) => candidate.name === name || candidate.id === name);
if (!service) process.exit(1);
const replicas = service.replicas || {};
console.log([service.status || "", replicas.running || 0, replicas.crashed || 0, replicas.exited || 0, replicas.total || 0].join("\t"));
'
}

wait_for_deployment() {
  local service_name="$1"
  local deployment_id="$2"
  local deadline=$((SECONDS + 900))
  local deployment_json
  local deployment_line
  local deployment_status
  local skipped_reason
  local health_line
  local service_status
  local running_replicas
  local crashed_replicas
  local exited_replicas
  local total_replicas

  while true; do
    if deployment_json="$(
      railway deployment list \
        --project "$RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID" \
        --service "$service_name" \
        --environment production \
        --limit 10 \
        --json
    )"; then
      deployment_line="$(
        DEPLOYMENTS_JSON="$deployment_json" \
        DEPLOYMENT_ID="$deployment_id" \
        node -e 'const deployments = JSON.parse(process.env.DEPLOYMENTS_JSON || "[]"); const id = process.env.DEPLOYMENT_ID || ""; const deployment = deployments.find((candidate) => candidate.id === id); if (deployment) console.log([deployment.id, deployment.status, deployment.meta?.skippedReason || ""].join("\t"));'
      )"

      if [ -n "$deployment_line" ]; then
        IFS=$'\t' read -r deployment_id deployment_status skipped_reason <<< "$deployment_line"
        echo "Railway deployment $deployment_id is $deployment_status."

        case "$deployment_status" in
          SUCCESS)
            return 0
            ;;
          SKIPPED)
            echo "Railway skipped deployment: ${skipped_reason:-no reason provided}."
            health_line="$(service_health_line "$service_name" || true)"
            if [ -n "$health_line" ]; then
              IFS=$'\t' read -r service_status running_replicas crashed_replicas exited_replicas total_replicas <<< "$health_line"
              if [ "$service_status" = "SUCCESS" ] && [ "${running_replicas:-0}" -gt 0 ]; then
                return 0
              fi
            fi
            echo "Railway skipped deployment, but ${service_name} is not currently healthy."
            echo "Service status: ${service_status:-unknown}; replicas running=${running_replicas:-0}, crashed=${crashed_replicas:-0}, exited=${exited_replicas:-0}, total=${total_replicas:-0}."
            return 1
            ;;
          FAILED|CRASHED|REMOVED)
            print_deployment_logs "$service_name" "$deployment_id"
            return 1
            ;;
        esac
      else
        echo "Waiting for Railway deployment $deployment_id to appear..."
      fi
    else
      echo "Waiting for Railway deployment status..."
    fi

    if [ "$SECONDS" -ge "$deadline" ]; then
      echo "Timed out waiting for Railway deployment."
      return 1
    fi

    sleep 10
  done
}

deploy_message="$(deploy_message_from_event)"
deploy_output=""
deployment_id=""

ensure_service_exists "$service_name"

deploy_output="$(
  railway up "$upload_root" \
  --path-as-root \
  --no-gitignore \
  --project "$RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID" \
  --service "$service_name" \
  --environment production \
  --message "$deploy_message" \
  --detach \
  --json
)"
printf '%s\n' "$deploy_output"

deployment_id="$(
  DEPLOY_OUTPUT="$deploy_output" node -e '
const lines = (process.env.DEPLOY_OUTPUT || "").split(/\r?\n/).filter(Boolean);
for (const line of lines) {
  try {
    const parsed = JSON.parse(line);
    if (parsed && typeof parsed.deploymentId === "string" && parsed.deploymentId.length > 0) {
      process.stdout.write(parsed.deploymentId);
      process.exit(0);
    }
  } catch {}
}
process.exit(1);
'
)"

wait_for_deployment "$service_name" "$deployment_id"

payload.message:
Read-only code-reaper audit complete. No P0 findings.

### P1 — Finish deleting the unreachable pending-import workflow

The staged-update workflow was removed in commit `5c92a3f`, but a large vertical slice remains.

Evidence:

- The only constructor is test-only: [artifacts.rs](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/git/import/artifacts.rs:19) gates `pending_import_from_staging_repo` with `#[cfg(test)]`.
- Production initializes the field to `None`: [store.rs](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/store.rs:254).
- Repository-wide search found no production assignment of `Some(PendingImport)`; normal updates only clear it at [reviewed_updates.rs](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/reviewed_updates.rs:184).
- Nevertheless, the impossible state remains modeled by `PendingImportFile`, `PendingImport`, and `StoredRepository.pending_import` at [store.rs](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/store.rs:127), persisted as a JSON column at [repositories.rs](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/schema_contract/repositories.rs:14), and supported by the dead publish operation [publish_apply.rs](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/publish_apply.rs:15).
- It leaks outward as `pending_import_pending` through [repo_reads.rs](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/repo_reads.rs:41), [responses.rs](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/responses.rs:203), [api.rs](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/cli/src/api.rs:152), CLI branching in [push.rs](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/cli/src/push.rs:50), and UI branching in [repo-primary-action.tsx](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/src/components/repo-primary-action.tsx:50).
- Several tests manufacture this otherwise unreachable state, especially `push_intent_completion.rs` and `git_receive.rs`.

Simplest cleanup: destructively remove `PendingImport*`, the repository field/column/entity serialization, pending-import projection/read/publish branches, response/CLI/web boolean, and tests that construct it. First push already publishes directly via [reviewed_updates.rs](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/reviewed_updates.rs:186).

### P1 — Delete the unowned repository-settings subsystem

`RepoSettings` is persisted and tested, but neither field affects production behavior.

Evidence:

- The two fields are defined at [store.rs](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/store.rs:156): `include_ignored_files` and `review_pushes_before_applying`.
- Repository-wide searches found no production behavior reading either field. `review_pushes_before_applying` appears only in persistence/read DTO plumbing and tests; `include_ignored_files` appears only in persistence.
- `MetadataStore::update_repo_settings` at [repo_settings.rs](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/repo_settings.rs:13) has no caller outside `api/src/tests/repo_lifecycle.rs`.
- `MetadataStore::repo_settings` at [repo_reads.rs](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/repo_reads.rs:126) likewise has no production caller.
- Despite this, every repository gets a separate `scope_repository_settings` table row defined at [repositories.rs](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/schema_contract/repositories.rs:42), an entity at [entities/repositories.rs](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/entities/repositories.rs:83), fact-row load/save plumbing, a DB behavior module, and a read DTO exported from [db/mod.rs](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/mod.rs:54).
- There is no settings HTTP route; `/v1/repos/.../settings` is explicitly asserted absent.

Simplest cleanup: delete `RepoSettings`, the settings table/entity/fact-row plumbing, `db/repo_settings.rs`, `RepoSettingsRead` and its query, `repo_actions::update_settings`, and tests of the nonexistent subsystem. Default visibility already belongs to `RepoConfig`, the current source of truth.

### P2 — Remove manifest and feature drift

Unused direct dependencies verified by exact source search:

- [api/Cargo.toml](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/Cargo.toml:17): `chacha20poly1305`
- [api/Cargo.toml](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/Cargo.toml:26): `sea-orm`
- [api/Cargo.toml](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/Cargo.toml:27): `sea-orm-migration`
- [api/Cargo.toml](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/Cargo.toml:32): `similar`
- [api/Cargo.toml](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/Cargo.toml:34): `sqlx`
- [api/Cargo.toml](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/Cargo.toml:35): `thiserror`
- [scope-core/Cargo.toml](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/Cargo.toml:36): `tokio-stream`

For each, the dependency declaration was the only match in that crate’s source boundary. The database/crypto implementations now live in `scope-core`; these appear to be move residue.

Feature aliases are also redundant:

- API `local-dev` activates both API `memory-metadata` and `scope_core/local-dev` at [api/Cargo.toml](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/Cargo.toml:10).
- Core `local-dev` is only an alias for `memory-metadata` at [scope-core/Cargo.toml](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/Cargo.toml:10).
- No code or command refers to API `memory-metadata` or core `local-dev` independently.

Simplest cleanup: make API `local-dev = ["scope_core/memory-metadata"]`; remove the two unused aliases and unused dependencies.

### P2 — Remove duplicated Railway upload trees

Both web and CLI deployment jobs stage a usable upload root, then copy the whole project again into an unused nested directory.

- Web copies `web/.` to `.railway-upload/`, then repeats it under `.railway-upload/web/`: [scope-web-ci.yml](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/.github/workflows/scope-web-ci.yml:94).
- CLI constructs the complete root at lines 247–251, then repeats the CLI and `scope-core` under `.railway-upload/cli`: [scope-cli-build.yml](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/.github/workflows/scope-cli-build.yml:242).
- Deployment explicitly treats `.railway-upload` as root via `railway up ... --path-as-root`: [deploy-railway.sh](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/.github/scripts/deploy-railway.sh:175).
- Root `web/railpack.json` builds from `.`; root `cli/railpack.json` runs `./scripts/build-railway.sh .`. Neither references nested `web/` or `cli/`.

Simplest cleanup: delete web lines 100–101 and CLI lines 252–262. Keep only one staged root and one validation path.

### P2 — Delete the test that preserves names of obsolete routes

[obsolete_routes.rs](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/tests/obsolete_routes.rs:3) permanently records eight pre-release route names solely to assert they remain 404, including the dead settings/pending-import/publish workflow. This is historical compatibility knowledge with no user contract.

Simplest cleanup: delete the file and its registration at [tests/mod.rs](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/tests/mod.rs:62). Router positive tests already establish the supported surface.

### P3 — Clean stale suppressions/config

- [staging.rs](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/git/import/staging.rs:16) and [staging.rs](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/git/import/staging.rs:32) apply `#[allow(dead_code)]` to symbols that are currently used by `artifacts.rs`/`import.rs`. Remove the stale suppressions so future dead code is visible.
- [doctor.config.json](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/doctor.config.json:7) exempts `src/routes/repos.$owner.$repo.review.tsx`, which does not exist. Remove the obsolete override.
- `docs/superpowers/specs/` contains 21 historical design/implementation artifacts totaling 13,657 lines and about 490 KB. No runtime, build, README, or CI path references them; only documents cross-reference one another. If Git history is sufficient, delete completed plans or retain only current architecture/decision records. These are historical artifacts, not generated source.

Intentional retained/generated files:

- [routeTree.gen.ts](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/src/routeTree.gen.ts) is generated but required by `router.tsx`.
- [types.generated.ts](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/src/api/types.generated.ts) is generated, imported by the web API layer, and drift-checked by `check-api-contract.mjs`.
- Root `Cargo.lock` and `cli/Cargo.lock` are intentionally separate because `cli` is excluded from the root workspace.
- `components.json`, `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, Railpack manifests, benchmark/dev tooling, and `.scope` metadata all have current tool or documented workflow owners; I found no basis to delete them.

Read-only code-reaper audit complete. No P0 findings.

## P1 — Finish deleting the unreachable pending-import workflow

The staged-update workflow was removed in commit `5c92a3f`, but a large vertical slice remains.

Evidence:

- Its only constructor, `pending_import_from_staging_repo`, is test-only at `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/git/import/artifacts.rs:19`.
- Production initializes `StoredRepository.pending_import` to `None` at `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/store.rs:254`.
- Repository-wide search found no production assignment of `Some(PendingImport)`; normal updates only clear it at `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/reviewed_updates.rs:184`.
- The impossible state is still modeled by `PendingImportFile`, `PendingImport`, and `StoredRepository.pending_import` at `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/store.rs:127-143,222`.
- It is persisted as a JSON column at `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/schema_contract/repositories.rs:14` and supported by the dead publish operation at `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/publish_apply.rs:15`.
- It leaks outward as `pending_import_pending` through:
  - `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/repo_reads.rs:41`
  - `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/http/responses.rs:203`
  - `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/cli/src/api.rs:152`
  - CLI branching at `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/cli/src/push.rs:50`
  - UI branching at `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/src/components/repo-primary-action.tsx:50`
- Tests such as `api/src/tests/push_intent_completion.rs` and `api/src/tests/git_receive.rs` manufacture this otherwise unreachable state.

Simplest cleanup: remove `PendingImport*`, the repository field/column/entity serialization, pending-import projection/read/publish branches, response/CLI/web boolean, and tests that construct it. First push already publishes directly at `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/reviewed_updates.rs:186`.

## P1 — Delete the unowned repository-settings subsystem

`RepoSettings` is persisted and tested, but neither field affects production behavior.

Evidence:

- `include_ignored_files` and `review_pushes_before_applying` are defined at `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/domain/store.rs:156-168`.
- Exact searches found no production behavior reading either field. `review_pushes_before_applying` appears only in persistence/read plumbing and tests; `include_ignored_files` appears only in persistence.
- `MetadataStore::update_repo_settings` at `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/repo_settings.rs:13` has no caller outside `api/src/tests/repo_lifecycle.rs`.
- `MetadataStore::repo_settings` at `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/repo_reads.rs:126` has no production caller.
- Every repository nevertheless gets a separate table defined at `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/schema_contract/repositories.rs:42`, an entity at `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/entities/repositories.rs:83`, fact-row plumbing, a DB behavior module, and an exported read DTO at `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/src/db/mod.rs:54`.
- There is no settings HTTP route; the old settings route is explicitly asserted absent.

Simplest cleanup: delete `RepoSettings`, the settings table/entity/fact-row plumbing, `db/repo_settings.rs`, `RepoSettingsRead` and its query, `repo_actions::update_settings`, and tests of the nonexistent subsystem. Default visibility already belongs to `RepoConfig`.

## P2 — Remove manifest and feature drift

Unused direct dependencies verified by exact source search:

- `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/Cargo.toml:17` — `chacha20poly1305`
- `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/Cargo.toml:26` — `sea-orm`
- `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/Cargo.toml:27` — `sea-orm-migration`
- `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/Cargo.toml:32` — `similar`
- `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/Cargo.toml:34` — `sqlx`
- `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/Cargo.toml:35` — `thiserror`
- `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/Cargo.toml:36` — `tokio-stream`

For each, its declaration was the only match in that crate’s source boundary.

Feature aliases are also redundant:

- API `local-dev` activates both API `memory-metadata` and `scope_core/local-dev` at `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/Cargo.toml:10`.
- Core `local-dev` is only an alias for `memory-metadata` at `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/crates/scope-core/Cargo.toml:10`.
- No code or documented command refers to API `memory-metadata` or core `local-dev` independently.

Simplest cleanup: use `local-dev = ["scope_core/memory-metadata"]`; remove the two aliases and unused dependencies.

## P2 — Remove duplicated Railway upload trees

Both web and CLI deployment jobs stage a usable upload root, then copy the entire project again into an unused nested directory.

- Web repeats the project under `.railway-upload/web/` at `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/.github/workflows/scope-web-ci.yml:94-101`.
- CLI constructs the complete root at lines 247-251, then repeats CLI and `scope-core` under `.railway-upload/cli` at `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/.github/workflows/scope-cli-build.yml:242-262`.
- Deployment explicitly treats `.railway-upload` as root using `--path-as-root` at `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/.github/scripts/deploy-railway.sh:175-177`.
- Root Railpack commands build from `.`; neither manifest references the nested copy.

Simplest cleanup: delete web lines 100-101 and CLI lines 252-262, keeping one staged root and one validation path.

## P2 — Delete the obsolete-route tombstone test

`/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/tests/obsolete_routes.rs:3-31` permanently records eight pre-release route names solely to assert they remain 404, including the dead settings, pending-import, and publish workflow. This is historical compatibility knowledge without a user contract.

Simplest cleanup: delete the file and its registration at `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/tests/mod.rs:62`. Positive router tests establish the supported surface.

## P3 — Clean stale suppressions and configuration

- `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/api/src/git/import/staging.rs:16` and `:32` apply `#[allow(dead_code)]` to symbols currently used by `artifacts.rs`/`import.rs`. Remove the stale suppressions.
- `/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/doctor.config.json:7` exempts `src/routes/repos.$owner.$repo.review.tsx`, which does not exist. Remove the override.
- `docs/superpowers/specs/` contains 21 historical design/implementation artifacts totaling 13,657 lines and about 490 KB. No runtime, build, README, or CI path references them; only documents cross-reference one another. If Git history is sufficient, delete completed plans or retain only current architecture/decision records.

## Intentionally retained/generated

- `web/src/routeTree.gen.ts` is generated but imported by `router.tsx`.
- `web/src/api/types.generated.ts` is generated, imported by the web API layer, and drift-checked by `check-api-contract.mjs`.
- Root `Cargo.lock` and `cli/Cargo.lock` are intentionally separate because `cli` is excluded from the root workspace.
- `components.json`, `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, Railpack manifests, benchmark/dev tooling, and `.scope` metadata have current tool or documented workflow owners; I found no verified basis to delete them.
